prompt: Tight macro three-quarter detail shot of a smartphone resting on a leather work boot toe at a job site, screen on. The phone screen displays a generic government-website-style page layout — a header bar with an abstract circular state-seal style emblem (no legible heraldry, no readable letters), several rows of form-field rectangles in pale gray, a single highlighted button in the screen's center — but every text element is intentionally rendered as pure visual blocks, no readable letters anywhere on the screen. Shot with a 100mm macro lens, very shallow depth of field — the screen tack-sharp, the boot leather softly defocused. Single cool overhead workshop fluorescent reflects subtly on the screen glass. Slightly desaturated palette, the brand red on a tape measure clipped to the boot's belt loop is the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. Absolutely no readable text on or off the screen.
